videos.description: The American Robin is one of the most familiar birds in North America. Its cheerful song — a series of liquid whistles — is often the first birdsong heard in the morning.
The brilliant red plumage of the male Northern Cardinal is unmistakable. Both sexes sing loud, clear whistles that can be heard year-round, even in winter.
Named for its distinctive 'chick-a-dee-dee-dee' call, this tiny, acrobatic bird adds more 'dees' when predators are near — a natural alarm system.
